channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Padam Cotton Yarns Boosts Capital by ₹9 Crore as Bon Lon Takes 6.83% Stake
item.description: Bon Lon Private Limited will acquire a 6.83% stake in Padam Cotton Yarns Ltd. via a rights issue planned for March 18, 2026. This move will boost Padam Cotton's equity share capital by more than ₹9 crore, signaling a significant capital injection for the yarn maker.

item.title: India Private Credit Shines Amid US Turmoil, Fueled by Domestic Demand
item.description: India's private credit market, valued at an estimated $25-30 billion with robust 35% annual growth, stands insulated from the current US liquidity crunch. Structural differences, including closed-end fund mandates and stringent SEBI regulations against leverage, create a strong buffer against the rising defaults and redemption pressures seen in US funds. While foreign portfolio investor (FPI) spillover risk is deemed limited, the sector's future hinges on sustained domestic capital inflows and vigilant management of evolving valuation trends.

item.title: Samvardhana Motherson Declares ₹0.35 Interim Dividend for FY26
item.description: Samvardhana Motherson International Limited's board has approved an interim dividend of ₹0.35 per equity share for the financial year 2025-26. The company has set March 27, 2026, as the record date to determine eligible shareholders. This move signals confidence in the company's financial health and commitment to returning value to its investors.

item.title: Glottis Ltd Launches Malaysia Unit to Boost Freight Services
item.description: Glottis Limited's Board of Directors has approved the establishment of a wholly-owned freight forwarding subsidiary in Malaysia, marking a strategic move to broaden its operational footprint and enhance regional customer service. This expansion, approved on March 19, 2026, involves an initial investment of up to USD 5,000.

item.title: Kothari Industrial Adds MD's Daughter to Board, Shareholder Vote Pending
item.description: Kothari Industrial Corporation Limited has appointed Ms. Rafiq Ahmed Sulaiha Banu as an Additional Director, effective March 20, 2026. The appointment, which requires shareholder approval via postal ballot, brings the MD's daughter onto the board, continuing a trend of promoter family involvement in leadership. This move follows recent board changes and a capital infusion.

item.title: SAMIL, Hellmann Worldwide Logistics launch global auto logistics joint venture
item.description: Samvardhana Motherson International (SAMIL) has approved a significant joint venture with German logistics giant Hellmann Worldwide Logistics. This strategic alliance, to be incorporated in Dubai, aims to provide world-class supply chain solutions for the global automotive industry, enhancing resilience and efficiency. SAMIL will hold a 51% stake in the JV, marking a key expansion for its logistics division.

item.title: India Railways Attracts $942M FDI, Eyes Global Growth with Major Funding Boost
item.description: Indian Railways attracted USD 942 million in Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) equity from FY15 to Dec 2025. This is supported by Gross Budgetary Support (GBS) rising to ₹2.78 lakh crore for FY27. This funding is modernizing infrastructure, boosting manufacturing for exports (over USD 3,355 million), and positioning India in global rail development. The sector's growth impacts listed firms like RVNL and IRFC, though their valuations differ.

item.title: India Tax Overhaul Boosts Manufacturing, Improves Cash Flow from April 2026
item.description: India's indirect tax system undergoes a major overhaul from April 1, 2026, with strategic changes in customs duties, GST, and trade facilitation. The Finance Bill, 2026, signals a pivot to support domestic manufacturing through targeted duty increases on key components and aims to improve business liquidity by expanding deferred customs duty payments. This reset seeks to rationalize exemptions, enhance transparency, and bolster India's industrial base while streamlining compliance.

item.title: Electrosteel Castings: Promoter Group Buys Shares, Ups Stake to 49.53%
item.description: Electrosteel Thermal Coal Limited, acting for the promoter group, acquired more than 2 crore shares in Electrosteel Castings Limited via open market deals. This boosts the promoter group's total stake to 49.53% from 46.21%, signaling greater confidence and control.

item.title: India's Renewable Push Faces Coal and Import Risks
item.description: India plans a major transformation of its power sector, targeting 1,121 GW capacity by 2035-36, led by solar energy. However, a Central Electricity Authority report indicates ongoing reliance on coal for baseload power and risks importing critical battery minerals and technology. The sector needs $2.2 trillion investment, with energy storage crucial for grid stability.

item.title: TCS Partners ABB, Stock Hits 52-Week Low Amid Market Drop
item.description: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) has partnered with ABB on a strategic agreement for IT infrastructure, AI, and engineering solutions to boost industrial innovation. The deal combines ABB's automation strengths with TCS's AI expertise. However, TCS stock dropped to a 52-week low of ₹2,350.20, closing at ₹2,362.80 on March 19, 2026. This reflects a nearly 27% year-to-date decline, showing investor worries about the IT sector's pressures.

item.title: OpNet Brings Native DeFi to Bitcoin Mainnet, No Wrapped BTC Needed
item.description: OpNet has launched on Bitcoin's mainnet, enabling native decentralized finance applications. This breakthrough allows Bitcoin holders to access DeFi protocols like trading and yield generation without wrapping BTC or leaving its base layer, significantly mitigating counterparty risks previously associated with Bitcoin DeFi attempts.

item.title: Coinbase Prepares for Stablecoin Yield Shift Amid CLARITY Act Progress
item.description: The U.S. CLARITY Act is close to a deal on stablecoin yields, prompting Coinbase (COIN) to plan changes. The exchange is exploring non-interest rewards to keep users engaged. While some analysts worry about competition and revenue, Coinbase's strong overall business, trading, and ecosystem offer a solid base, supported by a 'Moderate Buy' rating.

item.title: Middle East Tensions Hike Indian Airline Costs, Squeeze Margins
item.description: The Airline Pilots' Association of India's (ALPA India) recent safety advisory highlights escalating risks in Middle East conflict zones. This advisory signals a broader financial crisis for Indian carriers, as war-risk insurance premiums surge and longer flight paths inflate operational costs. Airlines face diminished profitability, potential fare hikes, and softening demand, prompting analyst caution and calls for government intervention.

item.title: Suraj Industries Boosts Stake in Shri Gang to 20.02%
item.description: Suraj Industries has approved buying 1,40,000 equity shares in Shri Gang Industries, which operates in the breweries and distilleries sector. The acquisition will raise Suraj's stake from 19.31% to 20.02%, aiming to strengthen its position in the alcoholic beverages market. Shri Gang reported ₹352.62 crore in turnover and ₹29.33 crore in profit after tax for FY24-25. The transaction, considered arm's length, is expected to finish within 30 days.

item.title: Compuage Infocom Schedules March 25 Creditors Meeting Amid Insolvency
item.description: Compuage Infocom Limited has scheduled its 24th meeting for its creditors' committee on March 25, 2026. The company is undergoing a court-ordered insolvency resolution process. Resolution Professional Mr. Gajesh Labhchand Jain is managing its affairs, and this meeting is a significant step in the ongoing proceedings.

item.title: NTPC, Octopus Energy Pact for Clean Energy and Digital Tech
item.description: India's NTPC Limited has signed a non-binding MoU with the UK's Octopus Energy Group to explore collaborations in clean energy, digital platforms, renewable energy, EV charging infrastructure, and innovation. The partnership aims to boost efficiency and affordability in India and other regions, supporting India's energy transition goals.

item.title: West Asia Conflict Pushes Travel Insurers to Rethink Policies
item.description: Global conflicts like the one in West Asia are pushing travel insurers to rethink their policies. Claims for travel disruptions are rising, but the overall financial hit is low as fewer people travel to the region. Standard policies usually don't cover war, so travelers and insurers are reviewing terms.

item.title: SAIL Appoints T.N. Natarajan as Director (Commercial); Drives Market Strategy
item.description: Steel Authority of India Limited (SAIL) has appointed Shri T.N. Natarajan as its new Director (Commercial), effective March 19, 2026. With over 32 years of experience within SAIL across production, logistics, sales, and marketing, Natarajan's expertise is expected to steer SAIL's commercial direction amidst a growing Indian steel market and ongoing capacity expansion plans. This move signals a focus on enhancing market outreach and customer engagement.

item.title: Ujjivan SFB Boosts Lending Amid Bihar Ordinance, Global Risks
item.description: Ujjivan Small Finance Bank (SFB) is speeding up growth in secured lending, including gold loans, while managing a 40% customer overlap with its microfinance portfolio. Management sees progress in growth and asset quality, expecting little disruption from seasonal trends or elections. However, the bank faces challenges from the Bihar MFI Ordinance and easing MFIN rules, plus global geopolitical worries. Analysts mostly keep a BUY rating with a ₹72 target price, but valuation and universal bank license progress remain concerns.

item.title: Hercules Investments Names CA Mahendrakumar Gohel Independent Director
item.description: Hercules Investments appointed chartered accountant Mahendrakumar Amritlal Gohel as an Independent Director for five years. The role begins March 19, 2026, pending shareholder approval, to bolster board expertise.

item.title: BharatPe Appoints New POS Chief to Boost Offline Payments
item.description: BharatPe has appointed Himanshu Verma as the new Head of its Point-of-Sale (POS) business, intensifying its focus on offline payments and merchant expansion. This strategic move aims to solidify its physical payment infrastructure, a segment that remains significant despite UPI's widespread adoption. Verma brings over two decades of experience to lead merchant acquisition, distribution, and scaling of the POS vertical, reporting to CEO Nalin Negi. The company processed over ₹27,000 crore annually via its POS devices, complementing its substantial UPI transaction volumes.

item.title: India Mandates Energy Data as National Security Priority
item.description: India has now classified energy data as a national security concern. All companies in the oil and gas sector must provide detailed operational reports to the Petroleum Planning and Analysis Cell (PPAC). This new rule, part of the Petroleum and Natural Gas (Furnishing of Information) Order, 2026, removes confidentiality requirements and demands frequent, potentially daily, updates from refiners, importers, and distributors. The goal is to create a live view of supply chains to prevent disruptions, especially those caused by Middle East conflicts and issues at key routes like the Strait of Hormuz. This will help the government better manage essential supplies and make smarter procurement choices during crises.

item.title: ITC Hotels Issues 93,421 Shares on ESOP Exercise, Boosting Capital
item.description: ITC Hotels Limited has issued 93,421 equity shares after employees exercised their stock options. The allotment, valued at ₹1 per share, slightly increased the company's total paid-up capital, continuing its employee incentive programs since its demerger from ITC Limited.

item.title: JCT Ltd Creditors Meet March 20 on Insolvency Path
item.description: JCT Limited will hold its 13th Committee of Creditors (CoC) meeting on March 20, 2026, via video conference. This is a critical stage in its Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) as stakeholders gather to decide the textile firm's future, potentially paving the way for its revival or liquidation.

item.title: Isgec Heavy Engineering Gets Independent ESG Score of 54
item.description: Isgec Heavy Engineering Limited has received an independent ESG rating of 54 from SEBI-licensed provider CFC Finlease Private Limited. The rating, based on publicly available data and not commissioned by the company, was disclosed on March 19, 2026. This assessment offers stakeholders insight into the company's environmental, social, and governance performance.

item.title: Salem Erode Cuts Rights Issue to ₹22.93 Cr for Board Review
item.description: Salem Erode Investments will hold a board meeting on March 24, 2026, to consider and re-approve a rights issue. The company plans to reduce the proposed issue size to ₹22.93 crore from the previously approved ₹50 crore, aiming to comply with new, simplified SEBI regulations and streamline fundraising.

item.title: MSP Steel & Power Credit Rating Lifted to BBB+ Stable
item.description: MSP Steel & Power Limited’s credit rating has been upgraded by CareEdge Ratings. Long-term facilities are now rated BBB+ Stable, up from BBB, and short-term facilities are A2, up from A3+. The upgrades acknowledge improved financial strength and consistent operational performance.

item.title: Oil Surge Sends India Stocks Down 3%, But Some Companies Hit 52-Week Highs
item.description: India's stock market fell sharply by over 3% on March 19, 2026. Rising crude oil prices nearing $117 per barrel and hawkish U.S. Federal Reserve signals drove the sell-off, with the Nifty 50 closing at 23,002.15 and the Sensex at 74,207.24. Despite the broad decline, some stocks, including recently listed APSISAERO and electrical maker PARTH, reached 52-week highs.

item.title: Sebi Board to Weigh FPI Trade Netting, Easier REITs Rules
item.description: India's market regulator, Sebi, will consider key rule changes at its March 23rd board meeting. Proposed measures include allowing foreign portfolio investors (FPIs) to net their cash market trades, easing investment rules for Real Estate and Infrastructure Investment Trusts (REITs/InvITs), and relaxing compliance for market intermediaries. While aimed at boosting market efficiency, the proposals also raise questions about potential risks.

item.title: Nestlé India Invests ₹225 Crore to Boost Munch Production Amid Margin Squeeze
item.description: Nestlé India is investing ₹225 crore to expand production of its 'Munch' brand at its Sanand factory. The expansion aims to add 8,300 tonnes annually by fiscal year 2026. This move follows a strong December quarter, where net profit surged 46% and revenue grew 18.5%. However, profit margins narrowed by 2 percentage points to 21.1%, signaling cost pressures. The company's stock dipped 1.23% to ₹1,190 before the announcement, despite an 8.97% gain over the past year.

item.title: India Insurers Shift Focus: Data, Design Over Disputes
item.description: India's insurance regulators and industry leaders are shifting from resolving disputes to actively designing the market. The focus is now on using claims data and digital tools like the NHCX to influence pricing and product development. Key initiatives include creating standardized 'basic insurance products' to lower complexity and improve affordability, while also combating high medical inflation. This shift aims for more transparency and efficiency in health insurance.

item.title: Venus Protocol Loses $3.7M in Exploit Via Price Manipulation
item.description: Venus Protocol lost $3.7 million after a price manipulation attack on the Thena (THE) token. The attacker used the token's low liquidity and Venus's price oracle to bypass limits over nine months. The exploit caused $2.15 million in bad debt, revealing ongoing risks in DeFi concerning how asset values are assessed and price feeds work.
